methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $375M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Quantinuum
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B

data.narrative:
Photonic Inc is a Series E quantum computing company founded by Stephanie Simmons and Paul Terry that has raised $375M CAD to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 7 private firms including Quantinuum, IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using $375M in total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation at Series E entry and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $525M. The peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-stage peer density in quantum computing; only one same-stage comparable exists, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold needed for reliable median estimation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $446M (low), $525M (median), and $604M (high), with the median representing the single methodologically sound output. Key uncertainties center on the applicability of stage-progression multipliers in an emerging sector with limited comparable financing data, and the absence of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers to validate relative valuation. Broader quantum computing sector comparables exist but were not integrated given the risk of cross-tier distortion.
